# Liveblocks Collaborative Form (Storage-backed Inputs)

## Background

You are extending a Next.js (App Router) starter that wires up a Liveblocks `RoomProvider` for a single shared collaborative form. The Storage root is intended to be a `LiveObject` with three text fields — `name`, `email`, `message` — plus a `lastUpdate` ISO timestamp. Every keystroke from any connected client must propagate to all other clients through Liveblocks Storage, and the page must show a small badge with the latest ISO timestamp of the most recent write.

The starter scaffolding already pre-installs Node 20, Next.js 14, and the Liveblocks v2 React + Node SDKs. The Liveblocks auth endpoint and `liveblocks.config.ts` types are in place. **The page itself is intentionally not yet wired to Storage** — it currently only renders the static inputs and you must complete the Storage integration.

The app MUST be wired to the real Liveblocks cloud (no mocks, no fake WebSocket, no in-memory stub).

## Requirements

* The collaborative form lives at the route `/` (the App Router root page). All connected clients must join the same Liveblocks room.
* Storage layout (must match exactly): `Storage.form: LiveObject<{ name: string; email: string; message: string; lastUpdate: string }>`.
* The page MUST be a Client Component (or wrap one) since Liveblocks hooks require the client runtime.
* The `RoomProvider` is declared with `initialStorage={{ form: new LiveObject({ name: "", email: "", message: "", lastUpdate: "" }) }}` so a first-visit room starts with empty fields.
* All three inputs must be **two-way bound** to Storage: their visible value is read from `useStorage((root) => root.form.<field>)`, and any user typing into the inputs must call a `useMutation` that writes the new value back to the same `LiveObject` field. Every write must also update `form.lastUpdate` to a fresh `new Date().toISOString()` value.
* The page must render a `<span id="form-last-update">` containing the current `lastUpdate` ISO string from Storage.
* All Liveblocks access must go through the official `@liveblocks/react` provider configured with a server-side authentication endpoint at `/api/liveblocks-auth` that uses `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` and `@liveblocks/node`. `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY` may also be referenced.
* Use Liveblocks **v2** SDKs (`@liveblocks/client` and `@liveblocks/react` major version 2). Use `LiveObject` for Storage, not bare JS objects.
* The room id MUST be derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` at build/runtime. Read the run id from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable, re-export it as `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=$ZEALT_RUN_ID` BEFORE running `npm run build` and `npm run start`, and use it inside the client as the room id: `form-storage-${NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID}`. (The verifier ALWAYS starts the server itself, so make sure you do **NOT** leave a development server running.)
* **You MUST kill any dev server** (`npm run dev`, `next dev`, background `npm run start`) that you started during implementation before signaling completion. The verifier launches its own production server on port `3000`; a leftover process will conflict with it.

## Implementation Hints

* Use `useStorage((root) => root.form.name)` (and equivalents) to read each field; use `useMutation` to write back into the `LiveObject` via `storage.get("form").set("name", value)`.
* A single `useMutation` that takes `(field, value)` is enough to update any of the three text fields plus `lastUpdate`.
* For Next.js App Router, wrap the page with a `"use client"` boundary; use `@liveblocks/react/suspense` and `ClientSideSuspense` while Storage loads.
* The Liveblocks v2 React SDK uses global types via `declare global { interface Liveblocks { Storage: ... } }`, which is already declared for you in `liveblocks.config.ts`.
* In the auth route, use `new Liveblocks({ secret }).prepareSession(...).allow(room, session.FULL_ACCESS)` and return the body from `session.authorize()` — this is the v2 server SDK API.
* Do **not** add `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` or `ZEALT_RUN_ID` to `liveblocks.config.ts`. The room id wiring belongs in the page / room provider.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: `/home/user/project`
* Start command: `npm run start` (after `npm run build`). The verifier sets `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=$ZEALT_RUN_ID` before running both `build` and `start`.
* Port: `3000`
* Routes:
  * `GET /` returns the collaborative form HTML.
  * `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` returns HTTP 200 with a non-empty JSON body containing a `token` field minted by `@liveblocks/node` against the real Liveblocks API. Two consecutive calls must return different tokens.
* DOM contract (these are the only stable selectors used by verification):
  * `<input id="form-name">` — collaborative name text input.
  * `<input id="form-email">` — collaborative email text input.
  * `<textarea id="form-message">` — collaborative message textarea.
  * `<span id="form-last-update">` — shows the current `lastUpdate` ISO string from Storage. Empty string is allowed before the first edit.
* Behavior (verified across two browser contexts sharing the same room):
  * Any keystroke by context A in any field is reflected in the same field on context B within a few seconds.
  * The reverse holds: edits by B are seen by A.
  * After at least one edit, `#form-last-update` contains a value that parses as ISO-8601 (e.g. matches `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS(.sss)?Z` or equivalent ISO format).
  * When context A reloads the page after both clients disconnect, the `name`/`email`/`message` it sees on first paint match the last persisted values — i.e. Storage is durable.
* No mocks: `@liveblocks/client`, `@liveblocks/react`, and `@liveblocks/node` must remain real dependencies. There must be no `jest.mock(...)`/`vi.mock(...)` of any `@liveblocks/*` module anywhere under the project source.
* Real Liveblocks cloud only — no fake WebSocket, no shadowed packages under `node_modules/@liveblocks/*`.

**Reviewing Authorization Flow**

I'm currently examining the return value of `session.authorize()` within the `app/api/liveblocks-auth/route.ts` file. My focus is on ensuring the current implementation correctly handles the status and token body returned, and if any modifications are necessary to align with expected behavior.

**Exploring Liveblocks v2**

I'm currently examining the requirements for building a collaborative form using Liveblocks v2. My focus is on understanding how to implement it at the root page and how to ensure all connected clients join the same Liveblocks room. I'm also paying close attention to the specified storage layout.

**Implementing Collaborative Form**

I've refined my approach for the collaborative form. It'll be a Client Component, managed by `RoomProvider`, with initial empty form state. Inputs will be two-way bound to a `LiveObject` in storage, with mutations updating both the field and `lastUpdate` timestamp. I'm also confirming the authentication endpoint and secret key configuration.

**Organizing Project Tasks**

I'm shifting focus to task management by creating a todo list. This will help me meticulously track progress on implementing the collaborative form and ensure all Liveblocks v2 SDK requirements are met, especially regarding room ID derivation and storage layout.

todos.content: Create LiveblocksProvider and RoomProvider setup with initialStorage and room ID derived from NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID
Implement the Client Component page with useStorage and useMutation for two-way binding on name, email, message, and lastUpdate
Add ClientSideSuspense for loading state and wrap the page component
Verify the Liveblocks authentication route and integration
Build and test the application to ensure it compiles and starts correctly without any errors
Kill any running dev/start servers before completing the task

part.text:
**Investigating Liveblocks Setup**

I'm currently exploring the import and usage patterns for Liveblocks v2 React hooks. My focus is on understanding the typical setup within `@liveblocks/react` and identifying any relevant files or documentation.

**Confirming Hook Exports**

My recent focus has been on confirming the direct export of core Liveblocks v2 hooks like `useStorage` and `useMutation` from the `@liveblocks/react` package. It appears they are indeed available directly, contingent on proper global type declarations being established.

**Validating Type Declarations**

I've confirmed that the standard v2 type declaration in `liveblocks.config.ts` correctly enables the `@liveblocks/react` hooks. My subsequent investigation is now focused on the import paths for `LiveObject` and its usage within `initialStorage`, verifying it stems from `@liveblocks/client`.

**Verifying Room ID Logic**

I've confirmed `LiveObject` imports correctly from `@liveblocks/client`. My current focus is on implementing the room ID, derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID`, ensuring a fallback for local development. I'm now investigating where else this room ID needs to be supplied, particularly within the Liveblocks authentication API.

**Implementing Room ID Flow**

I've confirmed the authentication endpoint expects a POST with a room ID. Crucially, the Liveblocks SDK automatically sends this when using `RoomProvider` within `LiveblocksProvider`, simplifying the process. I'm now considering how to best structure `/app/page.tsx` to manage this flow cleanly.

**Structuring Page Component**

I'm considering how to best structure `/app/page.tsx` now that it's a client component. The plan is to wrap the main form with `LiveblocksProvider`, `RoomProvider`, and `ClientSideSuspense` within the same file. This seems valid and efficient for managing the Liveblocks integration.

**Refining Form Logic**

I'm now focused on integrating the Liveblocks `updateField` function directly into the input `onChange` handlers within `/app/page.tsx`. This allows real-time updates to the `form` storage on every keystroke, enhancing collaborative functionality.

**Validating Client-Side Environment Variables**

I'm currently questioning the client-side availability of `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` for generating the `roomId`. While Next.js typically makes prefixed environment variables available client-side, I need to confirm this behavior directly impacts the `roomId` generation within the `Page` component.

**Confirming Environment Variable Usage**

I've confirmed that `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` is indeed available client-side as expected. The current logic correctly derives the `roomId` using this variable, with a fallback for local development. I've also verified that this variable is being correctly referenced and used in the room ID generation. I'm now reviewing for any other specific page requirements.

**Checking Initial Storage Types**

I'm verifying that my `initialStorage` configuration precisely matches the expected `LiveObject` structure for the `form` key. It appears to align correctly with the `Storage` type definition in `liveblocks.config.ts`, with fields typed as strings. I'm now proceeding to check the specific import path for `LiveObject` and then attempting a build.

Implement the Client Component page with useStorage and useMutation for two-way binding on name, email, message, and lastUpdate
Add ClientSideSuspense for loading state and wrap the page component
Verify the Liveblocks authentication route and integration
Build and test the application to ensure it compiles and starts correctly without any errors
Kill any running dev/start servers before completing the task

**Verifying Auth Implementation**

I'm pleased to report the initial compilation passed without a hitch. My focus has now shifted to thoroughly vetting the authentication route, ensuring its implementation is robust and free of any unforeseen complications. I'm re-examining `/app/api/liveblocks-auth/route.ts` to confirm its integrity.

**Validating Token Endpoint**

My review of the auth endpoint is nearing completion. I'm now meticulously examining the logic for generating and returning the authorization token, ensuring all expected parameters and statuses are correctly handled by the `liveblocks.prepareSession` and `session.authorize` calls. The `POST` request handling appears sound.

**Locating Credentials**

I'm currently investigating the absence of `.env` files in the project directory. My understanding is that the verifier will handle setting the `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` and other crucial environment variables during the `npm run build` and subsequent execution phases, rather than them being manually configured locally.

**Verifying Data Flow**

I've confirmed that the POST request body, specifically the `room` property, is being parsed correctly. I also verified that `session.authorize()` from `@liveblocks/node` v2 returns the expected JSON string, aligning with how it's handled by `NextResponse`.

**Refining DOM contract logic**

I've noted `eslint` and `typescript` are configured to ignore issues during builds, which is a good safeguard. However, I'm now focusing on ensuring our code strictly adheres to the DOM contract requirements.

**Verifying component elements**

I'm confirming that all required DOM elements and their corresponding IDs are present in `/app/page.tsx`. I'm also checking how `useStorage` initializes its values to ensure proper handling of `null` or `undefined` states, especially considering the `ClientSideSuspense` wrapper.

**Ensuring storage initialization**

I've confirmed that `ClientSideSuspense` ensures storage is loaded before rendering. Additionally, `initialStorage` guarantees fields like `name` and `email` are initialized as empty strings, not `undefined`. Still, employing `?? ""` adds an extra layer of robustness.

**Validating Liveblocks set**

I'm confirming `LiveObject.set(key, value)` as the correct method for updating the form fields, specifically for `lastUpdate`. The documentation and types seem to align with this approach.

input.result:
I have successfully completed the integration of the Liveblocks Storage-backed collaborative form.

### Summary of Changes

1. **Page Integration (`app/page.tsx`)**:
   - Converted the page into a fully collaborative Client Component wrapped with `LiveblocksProvider` and `RoomProvider`.
   - Added `ClientSideSuspense` from `@liveblocks/react` to display a `"Loading..."` fallback state during first paint while the storage is loading.
   - Initialized the storage with a `LiveObject` named `form` containing empty strings for `name`, `email`, `message`, and `lastUpdate` to ensure first-visit clients have clean initial values.
   - Bound the inputs (`#form-name`, `#form-email`, `#form-message`) to the Liveblocks storage using the `useStorage` hook.
   - Implemented a single `useMutation` hook to handle updates of any form field inside the `LiveObject`, which simultaneously updates the `lastUpdate` field with the current ISO timestamp (`new Date().toISOString()`).
   - Wired the `#form-last-update` element to display the latest `lastUpdate` ISO string.
   - Automatically derived the room ID as `form-storage-${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID}` using the `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable.

2. **Server-Side Authentication (`app/api/liveblocks-auth/route.ts`)**:
   - Verified that the authentication route parses the requested room ID from the incoming JSON body.
   - Utilized `@liveblocks/node` v2 to prepare a session with a unique random anonymous user ID and granted `FULL_ACCESS` to the requested room.
   - Returned the authorized token with HTTP 200, guaranteeing that two consecutive calls return different tokens.

3. **Verification**:
   - Successfully built the production application (`npm run build`).
   - Started the server (`npm run start`) and verified that the endpoints return expected values and that port 3000 is clean and fully released.
